title: Power BI - Period Slicer, Sync, Bookmarks, and Field Parameters for Security Dashboards
description: Production patterns for date slicers (between, relative, dropdown), syncing slicers across pages, default last-12-months behaviour, bookmarks for Monthly/Quarterly/Yearly view switching, and field parameters for dynamic axis switching

list_slicer.description: Shows all distinct values as a vertical or horizontal list. Users click one or multiple values.
list_slicer.best_for: MetricName, year, small number of periods. Not ideal for 24+ months.
list_slicer.setup: Add Slicer visual > drag DateTable[MonthYear] to Field > Format pane > Slicer settings > Style = List.

dropdown_slicer.description: Compact dropdown that shows selected value and expands on click. Best for space-constrained layouts.
dropdown_slicer.best_for: Month or quarter selection in a header bar alongside other slicers.
dropdown_slicer.setup: Format pane > Slicer settings > Style = Dropdown. Default: collapsed, shows selected value.

between_date_slider.description: A range slider for selecting a start and end date. Works only on Date-type columns.
between_date_slider.best_for: Ad-hoc date range selection by analysts.
between_date_slider.setup: Drag a Date column (DateTable[Date]) to the slicer field. Format pane > Slicer settings > Style = Between. Two date pickers appear.
between_date_slider.note: When used with a monthly snapshot model, selecting a range like Feb 15 – Apr 15 will include Feb 1 and Apr 1 snapshots (since snapshots are on the 1st). This is usually desired — the analyst sees all snapshots within the approximate range.

relative_date_slicer.description: Automatically filters to a rolling window relative to today: 'last N days/months/years'. Requires a Date column (not a text label).
setup: 1. Add Slicer visual > drag DateTable[Date] to Field
2. Format pane > Slicer settings > Style = Relative date
3. Configure: 'Last', enter number, select 'Months' (or Quarters, Years)
relative_date_slicer.last_12_months: Set to: Last 12 Months. This automatically rolls forward — on March 1, 2026, it shows April 2025 through March 2026. No manual update needed.
relative_date_slicer.for_monthly_snapshots: The relative date filter acts on DateTable[Date], which then filters the fact table through the relationship. Monthly snapshots on the 1st of each month within the 12-month window are included.

tile_button_slicer.description: Each value shows as a button/tile. Good for a small fixed set of options.
tile_button_slicer.best_for: Year selection (2023, 2024, 2025, 2026) or metric category buttons.
tile_button_slicer.setup: Format pane > Slicer settings > Style = Tile.

sync_slicers_across_pages.overview: Sync slicers allows a slicer on one page to control filters on other pages. Users change the month once and all report pages update.
setup_steps: 1. Select the slicer on the page where it is physically placed
2. View ribbon > Sync slicers — panel opens on the right
3. In the Sync slicers panel, each report page is listed
4. For each page: toggle 'Sync' (eye icon with sync arrows) to make that page respond to the slicer; toggle 'Visible' (eye icon) to show the slicer control on that page
5. A page with Sync=ON and Visible=OFF will filter correctly but won't show the slicer UI — use this for detail pages that receive context from a summary page slicer
6. Slicers on different pages can sync to each other only if they use the same field (same table[column])
sync_slicers_across_pages.group_name: In the Sync slicers panel, assign the same 'Group name' to slicers that should sync together across pages. Default: Power BI auto-assigns a group when you enable sync.
sync_slicers_across_pages.slicer_on_every_page_vs_synced: Option A: Place a visible slicer on every page, all synced. Users can see and change the period on any page. Option B: Place the slicer only on a Summary page (Visible=ON), sync to all other pages (Visible=OFF). Simpler layout on detail pages.
sync_slicers_across_pages.slicer_field_must_match: Syncing only works if both slicers filter the same column. If one slicer uses DateTable[MonthYear] and another uses DateTable[Date], they will not sync — change both to the same column.
sync_slicers_across_pages.reset_slicer_button: Create a bookmark with the slicer at its default state (last 12 months selected). Add a Button visual, set Action > Bookmark to the reset bookmark. Place on all pages for consistent reset behaviour.

default_last_12_months.method_1_relative_slicer: Use a Relative Date slicer (Last 12 Months) — it automatically adjusts as months pass. No bookmark needed. Best for most cases.
default_last_12_months.method_2_bookmark_default: Create a bookmark while the date slicer shows 'last 12 months' selected. Mark this bookmark as the 'Default bookmark' (right-click in Bookmarks pane > Set as default). When users open the report, the slicer starts at that state.
default_last_12_months.method_3_visual_filter: Add a visual-level filter on the matrix: DateTable[MonthSort] > Top N, 12, by DateTable[MonthSort] (DESC). This always shows the latest 12 months without requiring a slicer selection. The slicer is then supplementary for custom ranges.
default_last_12_months.method_4_report_level_filter: In Filters pane > Report level filters: DateTable[Date] is in the last 12 months (relative date). This pre-filters the entire report to 12 months. Users cannot override it unless the filter is set to visible.

bookmarks_for_view_switching.overview: Bookmarks capture the state of all visuals, slicers, and filters on a page (or across pages). Use them to build a Monthly / Quarterly / Yearly view switcher that changes the period grouping on charts without needing multiple separate reports.
bookmarks_for_view_switching.bookmark_definition: A bookmark saves: which visuals are visible/hidden, slicer selections, filter states, scroll position. It does NOT save visual data — the data always refreshes.

view_switcher_setup.approach: Create three separate visuals (or groups) — one configured for monthly columns, one for quarterly, one for yearly. Then use bookmarks to hide two and show one.
steps: 1. Build 3 matrix visuals on the same page:
Position them exactly on top of each other (same X/Y/Size)
2. Open View > Selection pane and View > Bookmarks pane
3. With Matrix A visible (B and C hidden in Selection pane), create Bookmark 'Monthly View'
4. Show Matrix B, hide A and C, create Bookmark 'Quarterly View'
5. Show Matrix C, hide A and B, create Bookmark 'Yearly View'
7. For each button: Format pane > Action > Type = Bookmark > Bookmark = the matching bookmark
8. Users click the buttons to switch views
view_switcher_setup.bookmark_scope: Set bookmarks to capture only 'Data and display' for the specific visuals (right-click bookmark > Edit > uncheck 'All visuals', then select specific visuals). This prevents the bookmark from resetting unrelated slicer selections.

bookmarks_for_view_switching.tooltip_toggle_pattern: The same bookmark pattern works for toggling between 'Show count' and 'Show percentage' views of the same data, or switching chart types.

field_parameters.overview: Field parameters (introduced in Power BI May 2022) let users dynamically swap which field is used on an axis or in a slicer. A single visual can show 'by Month', 'by Quarter', or 'by Year' based on a slicer selection — without bookmarks or hidden visuals.
field_parameters.enable_preview: If not visible: File > Options > Preview features > Field parameters (check it) > restart Power BI Desktop.
create_field_parameter: 4. Click Create — Power BI creates a new table with the parameter and auto-adds a slicer
field_parameters.use_in_visuals: In the chart visual, drag the parameter measure [Period Grouping] (or the parameter field) to the Axis field well instead of a specific date column. The visual now groups by whatever the slicer selects.
field_parameters.sort_order_in_parameter: Each entry in the field parameter table includes a sort-order column. If the field being used (MonthYear, QuarterLabel) has Sort by Column set correctly in the DateTable, sorting propagates through the parameter to the visual.
field_parameters.field_parameter_vs_bookmarks: Field parameters: dynamic, one slicer controls axis, cleaner UX, supported in Power BI Service. Bookmarks: work in all Power BI versions, more flexible (can hide entire visual groups), but more maintenance. Prefer field parameters when the switch is purely about the period granularity axis.
field_parameters.field_parameter_for_metric_switching: Same technique works for metric switching: parameter of measure references ([Sev5 Count], [Sev4 Count], [Alert Count]) lets users pick which metric to chart with a slicer — useful on a trend chart page.

cross_filter_control.default_behaviour: By default, clicking a data point in any visual cross-filters all other visuals on the page. For security dashboards this is usually desired — clicking a month in a trend chart highlights the same month in the matrix.
edit_interactions: 3. Each other visual shows icons: Filter (funnel), Highlight (partial), None (no cross-filter)
4. Click the icon on the target visual to set the interaction type
5. Format ribbon > Edit interactions again to exit
cross_filter_control.filter_vs_highlight: Filter: completely removes non-matching data from the target visual. Highlight: keeps all data but dims non-matching items. For security dashboards, Filter is usually clearer. Highlight can confuse totals because the visual still shows all data.
cross_filter_control.slicer_always_filters: Slicers always filter — the highlight/none options are not available for slicers as source.
cross_filter_control.prevent_slicer_from_filtering_specific_v: Select the target visual after clicking Edit interactions on the slicer > set to None. That visual will ignore the slicer. Useful for a 'reference' visual showing all-time data.

common_mistakes: Using a text column like DateTable[MonthYear] in a relative date slicer — relative date requires a Date-type column, not a text label. Use DateTable[Date] for relative date slicers.
Syncing slicers across pages using different columns (one uses [MonthYear], another uses [Date]) — they cannot sync. Standardise all slicers to the same column.
Bookmarks capturing all visuals and all data — if a user changes a slicer and then clicks a bookmark, the slicer resets. Scope bookmarks to specific visuals to avoid resetting unrelated filters.
Field parameters not showing sorted correctly — the sort key must be set on the underlying date column in the DateTable, not on the parameter table itself.
Forgetting to include the date slicer page in the Sync slicers group when adding new report pages — new pages start unsynchronised.
Setting 'Last 12 months' relative date filter at report level — analysts cannot then select a custom range. Use a page-level filter or slicer instead so it can be overridden.
Using the date range slider (between) with a text MonthYear column — slider requires numeric or date type. Use a List or Dropdown slicer for text labels.
